2604.13114.title: The Code Whisperer: LLM and Graph-Based AI for Smell and Vulnerability Resolution
2604.13114.paper_summary: Code smells and software vulnerabilities both increase maintenance cost, yet they are often handled by separate tools that miss structural context and produce noisy warnings. This paper presents The Code Whisperer, a hybrid framework that combines graph-based program analysis with large language models to detect, explain, and repair maintainability and security issues within a unified workflow. The method aligns Abstract Syntax Trees (ASTs), Control Flow Graphs (CFGs), Program Dependency Graphs (PDGs), and token-level code embeddings so that structural and semantic signals can be learned jointly. We evaluate the framework on multi-language datasets and compare it with rule-based analyzers and single-model baselines. The results indicate that the hybrid design improves detection performance and produces more useful repair suggestions than either graph-only or language-model-only approaches. We also examine explainability and CI/CD integration as practical requirements for adopting AI-assisted code review in everyday software engineering workflows.
2604.13114.paper_summary_zh: 程式碼異味和軟體漏洞都會增加維護成本，但它們通常由不同的工具處理，這些工具缺乏結構上下文，並產生嘈雜的警告。本文提出了The Code Whisperer，一個混合框架，將基於圖的程式分析與大型語言模型結合，以在統一的工作流程中檢測、解釋和修復可維護性和安全性問題。該方法對齊抽象語法樹（ASTs）、控制流圖（CFGs）、程式依賴圖（PDGs）和標記級別的程式碼嵌入，以便結構和語義信號可以共同學習。我們在多語言數據集上評估該框架，並將其與基於規則的分析器和單模型基準進行比較。結果表明，混合設計改善了檢測性能，並比僅使用圖或僅使用語言模型的方法產生更有用的修復建議。我們還檢查了解釋性和CI/CD整合作為在日常軟體工程工作流程中採用AI輔助程式碼審查的實際需求。
2604.13114.author: Mohammad Baqar et.al.
2604.13114.authors: Mohammad Baqar, Raji Rustamov, Alexander Hughes
